2605.24792.title: Parameter-Efficient VLMs for Gastrointestinal Endoscopy: Medical Image Generation and Clinical Visual Question Answering
2605.24792.paper_summary: The major limitations of gastrointestinal (GI) endoscopy AI systems arise from a shortage of annotated data, strict privacy policies, and significant bottlenecks in conventional model fine-tuning. Such limitations impede the successful application of sophisticated AI models in clinical practice, particularly affecting the reliability and scalability of diagnosis. In this paper, we present a dual-pipeline PEFT model that addresses two fundamental problems: medical Visual Question Answering (VQA) and the generation of privacy-preserving synthetic data. For clinical VQA, we adopt the Florence-2 vision-language model. Leveraging PEFT enhances model interpretability while substantially reducing the computational cost of training. Simultaneously, we employ Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) with Stable Diffusion 2.1 to generate high-quality GI images that enhance training databases without violating patient privacy. This research utilized the Kvasir-VQA dataset. Our Florence-2 VQA model achieved ROUGE-1 of 0.92, ROUGE-L of 0.91, and BLEU score improvements from 0.08 to 0.24. Fine-tuning on private datasets consistently showed better results than fine-tuning on public datasets. The rank-4 LoRA synthesis achieved optimal performance with a fidelity score of 0.290, an agreement score of 0.730, and a Frechet BiomedCLIP Distance (FBD) of 1450, reducing computational costs by almost 90 percent. This framework improves the clinical potential of AI in GI endoscopy. Compared to FLUX, MSDM, and Kandinsky 2.2, our model demonstrates superior FBD and strong semantic alignment. While other models lead in Fidelity or Agreement, our lower FBD indicates better image-text coherence. These results establish our approach as a robust solution for enhancing VQA and synthetic data generation in clinical AI.
2605.24792.paper_summary_zh: 胃腸道（GI）內視鏡AI系統的主要限制來自於標註數據的短缺、嚴格的隱私政策以及傳統模型微調中的重大瓶頸。<lb/>這些限制妨礙了先進AI模型在臨床實踐中的成功應用，特別影響了診斷的可靠性和可擴展性。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一種雙管道PEFT模型，解決了兩個基本問題：醫療視覺問答（VQA）和生成保護隱私的合成數據。<lb/>對於臨床VQA，我們採用了Florence-2視覺語言模型。<lb/>利用PEFT增強了模型的可解釋性，同時大幅降低了訓練的計算成本。<lb/>同時，我們使用低秩適應（LoRA）與穩定擴散2.1生成高品質的GI圖像，以增強訓練數據庫而不違反患者隱私。<lb/>本研究利用了Kvasir-VQA數據集。<lb/>我們的Florence-2 VQA模型達到了ROUGE-1的0.92、ROUGE-L的0.91，並且BLEU分數從0.08提升至0.24。<lb/>在私有數據集上的微調結果始終優於在公共數據集上的微調。<lb/>秩-4的LoRA合成達到了最佳性能，保真度分數為0.290，協議分數為0.730，Frechet BiomedCLIP距離（FBD）為1450，計算成本降低了近90%。<lb/>這一框架提高了AI在GI內視鏡中的臨床潛力。<lb/>與FLUX、MSDM和Kandinsky 2.2相比，我們的模型顯示出更優越的FBD和強大的語義對齊。<lb/>雖然其他模型在保真度或協議上領先，但我們較低的FBD指示了更好的圖像-文本一致性。<lb/>這些結果確立了我們的方法作為增強臨床AI中VQA和合成數據生成的穩健解決方案。
2605.24792.author: Ojonugwa Oluwafemi Ejiga Peter et.al.
2605.24792.authors: Ojonugwa Oluwafemi Ejiga Peter, Frederick Akor Ejiga, Fahmi Khalifa, Md Mahmudur Rahman
